skill.name: mcp-integration
skill.description: Guides plugin authors through adding Model Context Protocol servers to Claude Code plugins, including server types, authentication, tool naming, and testing.
skill.summary: Add MCP server integrations to Claude Code plugins with secure configuration patterns.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic skill reviewed with minimal audit scope. Static findings relate to MCP configuration examples for network URLs, environment variables, and local server commands, not hidden executable payloads. No prompt injection attempt or malicious instruction was detected.

content.user_title: Integrate MCP Servers into Claude Code Plugins
content.value_statement: Plugin authors need reliable ways to connect Claude Code to external services. This skill explains MCP server setup, authentication, tool permissions, and testing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to add MCP servers through .mcp.json or plugin.json.
Compares stdio, SSE, HTTP, and WebSocket MCP server types.
Shows authentication patterns for OAuth, headers, and environment variables.
Documents MCP tool naming and allowed-tools configuration for commands.
Provides testing and debugging steps using /mcp and Claude debug logs.
Includes example configurations for stdio, SSE, and HTTP servers.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a working MCP server implementation.
It does not verify that third-party MCP services are trustworthy.
It assumes users understand Claude Code plugin structure.
It cannot manage credentials or rotate tokens for users.

use_cases.title: Add a Hosted Service
use_cases.description: Configure an SSE or HTTP MCP endpoint so a plugin can use cloud service tools.

use_cases.title: Bundle a Local MCP Server
use_cases.description: Set up a stdio server that runs from the plugin directory and receives needed environment variables.

use_cases.title: Review MCP Permissions
use_cases.description: Define specific MCP tools for commands and avoid broad wildcard permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Server Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the right MCP server type for my Claude Code plugin. My service is [describe service], and users authenticate with [method].

prompt_templates.title: Create MCP Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an MCP configuration for a Claude Code plugin that connects to [service]. Use [stdio, SSE, HTTP, or WebSocket] and list required environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Secure Tool Permissions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned MCP tools for a Claude Code command. Recommend specific allowed tools and flag any wildcard or excessive permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Integration Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help debug my MCP integration. The server type is [type], the symptom is [error], and /mcp shows [status]. Provide likely causes and tests.

output_examples.input: User asks how to connect a plugin to a hosted task service with OAuth.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: use an SSE MCP server, rely on Claude Code OAuth handling, document first-use authentication, and test visibility with /mcp.

output_examples.input: User asks how to expose a local database helper to a plugin.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: use a stdio MCP server, reference plugin-relative paths, pass the database URL through environment variables, and log only to stderr.

output_examples.input: User asks whether a command should allow every MCP tool from a server.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: allow only the needed tools. Use wildcards only when every server tool is required for the command workflow.

best_practices: Use plugin-relative paths and document every required environment variable.
Prefer HTTPS or WSS for remote MCP servers and avoid hardcoded credentials.
Pre-allow specific MCP tools in commands and test integrations before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not commit API tokens or service credentials in MCP configuration files.
Do not use broad tool wildcards when a command needs only specific tools.
Do not rely on absolute local paths that break outside one developer machine.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you configure MCP servers for Claude Code plugins and connect them to external tools or services.

faq.question: Does it include a ready-made MCP server?
faq.answer: No. It provides configuration guidance, examples, and reference material for integrating servers you choose or build.

faq.question: Which MCP server types are covered?
faq.answer: It covers stdio, SSE, HTTP, and WebSocket servers, including when each type is useful.

faq.question: How should credentials be handled?
faq.answer: Use OAuth where supported or environment variables for tokens. Do not hardcode credentials in plugin files.

faq.question: Can commands call MCP tools automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. Commands can pre-allow specific MCP tools so Claude Code can use them during that command workflow.

faq.question: How do I test an MCP integration?
faq.answer: Install the plugin locally, run /mcp to inspect servers, test tool calls, and review Claude debug logs for failures.
